AskListResponse.description: Every ask still awaiting an answer.
AskListResponse.title: AskListResponse

AskRequest.description: A worker's ask: the question and its optional pipe-separated choices.
AskRequest.title: AskRequest

AskResponse.description: The recorded ask — its minted question id (openapi-ts consumes this).
AskResponse.title: AskResponse

AttachmentRequest.description: A worker's explicit artifact submission for one ``produces:`` name.
AttachmentRequest.title: AttachmentRequest

CapacitiesView.description: Agent slots — the same math FILL claims against.
CapacitiesView.title: CapacitiesView

EnvelopeArtifact.description: One artifact carried into a node-step, resolved latest-by-epoch.
EnvelopeArtifact.title: EnvelopeArtifact

EnvironmentListResponse.description: Every environment in the runner's configured pool, held or free.
EnvironmentListResponse.title: EnvironmentListResponse

EnvironmentView.description:
One environment in the runner's configured pool — ``GET /api/environments``
(issue #106). ``chunk_id``/``held_since`` are present only while the environment
is currently bound; an unused pool environment carries both as ``None``.
EnvironmentView.title: EnvironmentView

EscalationListResponse.description: Every escalation still open — no later lease mint has superseded it.
EscalationListResponse.title: EscalationListResponse

EscalationView.description: One parked escalation, carrying its literal takeover command — ``GET /api/escalations``.
EscalationView.title: EscalationView

FactListResponse.description: The most recent hub-bound facts, newest first.
FactListResponse.title: FactListResponse

FactView.description:
One hub-bound fact off the runner store's outbound buffer — ``GET /api/facts``.

The local fact log: the record itself minus its JSON ``payload`` (the panel
reads the ledger, not the bodies). ``acked_at`` null means still buffered.
FactView.title: FactView

FleetSummaryView.title: FleetSummaryView

HeartbeatRequest.description: A worker's heartbeat: the lease it inherited at spawn (``BLIZZARD_LEASE_ID``).
HeartbeatRequest.title: HeartbeatRequest

HubConnectivityView.description:
Hub reachability (derived, not probed) plus the outbound backlog depth.

``endpoint`` is the configured hub base URL (``RunnerConfig.hub_url``) — the
local panel's link out to the fleet board; connectivity facts, not a probe.
HubConnectivityView.title: HubConnectivityView

LeaseListResponse.description: Active leases, then recently-closed ones — the panel's list (issue #28/#29).
LeaseListResponse.title: LeaseListResponse

LeaseView.description:
One lease — active or recently-closed — with its joined binding facts and
derived state (issue #28; closed leases added issue #29).
LeaseView.title: LeaseView

OpenTakeoverListResponse.description: Every takeover still open across this runner's held chunks.
OpenTakeoverListResponse.title: OpenTakeoverListResponse

OpenTakeoverView.description:
One open operator takeover — ``GET /api/takeovers``, the stranded-takeover
recovery surface (issue #52): the chunk it holds, the ``takeover_id`` an
interrupted terminal never PATCHed closed, and how long it has been held.
OpenTakeoverView.title: OpenTakeoverView

PauseStateView.description: The pause brake's two independent surfaces, plus their effective OR.
PauseStateView.title: PauseStateView

PmItemEntry.description:
One pointer's pass-through PM item — title, body + comment
thread, vendor-native.

``label``/``web_url`` are the board-legible pointer label (``blizzard#8``) and its
browser address — both null when no configured source names ``source``. A
per-pointer forge failure degrades here rather than failing the whole read:
``error`` carries the reason and ``title``/``body`` are null, so one unreachable
pointer never blinds the reader to the pointers it did reach.
PmItemEntry.title: PmItemEntry

PmItemsView.description:
A chunk's pass-through PM items — one entry per pointer, order preserved.

Empty when the chunk holds no pointers — the board's empty state; a grouped chunk carrying
many pointers yields one entry per pointer, each fetched fresh and never stored.
PmItemsView.title: PmItemsView

ReadinessResponse.description: The wire shape of a readiness reading (openapi-ts consumes this).
ReadinessResponse.title: ReadinessResponse

RequeueResponse.description:
``POST /chunks/{id}/requeues`` — the local hold is cleared; the next FILL spawns
a fresh attempt at the chunk's current node.
RequeueResponse.title: RequeueResponse

RunnerControlPatch.description: Declarative controls on the runner singleton — ``paused`` now, routing knobs post-MVP.
RunnerControlPatch.title: RunnerControlPatch

RunnerControlView.description: The runner singleton's derived pause state (openapi-ts consumes this).
RunnerControlView.title: RunnerControlView

RunnerStatusView.description: ``GET /api/runner`` — identity, pause states, capacities, hub connectivity, last tick.
RunnerStatusView.title: RunnerStatusView

SelfTestCheckView.description: One check's pass/fail result (openapi-ts consumes this).
SelfTestCheckView.title: SelfTestCheckView

SelfTestStartRequest.description: Which coding harness to run the canary against.
SelfTestStartRequest.title: SelfTestStartRequest

SelfTestView.description: A selftest run's current state — ``running`` until every check has resolved.
SelfTestView.title: SelfTestView

TakeoverEndResponse.description: ``PATCH /chunks/{id}/takeovers/{tid}`` — the CLI calls this once its child exits.
TakeoverEndResponse.title: TakeoverEndResponse

TakeoverRequest.description: The takeover request body — ``force`` kills a live worker attempt first.
TakeoverRequest.title: TakeoverRequest

TranscriptResponse.description: A lease's parsed transcript — always 200 when the lease exists.
TranscriptResponse.title: TranscriptResponse

TurnView.description: One collapsed conversation turn on the wire.
TurnView.title: TurnView

WorkspacePromptReplacement.description: A replacement workspace prompt — applies to subsequent spawns with no restart.
WorkspacePromptReplacement.title: WorkspacePromptReplacement

WorkspacePromptResponse.description: The effective workspace prompt prepended to every worker spawn (openapi-ts consumes this).
WorkspacePromptResponse.title: WorkspacePromptResponse

get.description:
Every ask still awaiting an answer — ``GET /api/asks?open=true`` (issue #51).

Derived, hub-free: an ask reads open while its ``question_id`` carries no answer
fact (:meth:`~blizzard.runner.store.repository.IReadRunnerStore.open_asks`), whether
or not it has been forwarded to the hub yet. There is no closed-ask history to serve,
so ``open=false`` is refused rather than silently answered as if it were true.
Forward a chunk's PM-items read to the hub — the layered pass-through.

post.description:
Clear a needs_human chunk's local hold — the next FILL spawns a fresh attempt.

``409`` while the chunk's takeover is still open (end the interactive session first),
or while the chunk carries no open escalation (nothing needs_human to clear).

Open a takeover over a parked chunk with no running attempt (``409`` otherwise).

``force`` supersedes a live worker attempt instead of refusing: the runner kills it
after recording the takeover fact, fencing its in-flight submission at the hub as a
stale epoch, exactly like a reaped lease — but consuming no retry and recording no
escalation.

patch.description: Mark a takeover ended — the CLI calls this once its exec'd interactive child exits.

get.description: Every environment in this runner's configured pool, held or free.
Every escalation still open — no later lease mint has superseded it.
The newest ``limit`` hub-bound facts recorded by this runner, newest first.
Forward the fleet-summary read to the hub — the layered pass-through.

post.description: Record a lease heartbeat, stamped with the injected clock.

get.description: Active leases, then recently-closed ones, derived at read time (issue #28/#29).

The worker's own node-step inputs — every artifact resolved latest-by-epoch,
both kinds, kind-discriminated.

schema.title: Response List Artifacts Api Leases  Lease Id  Artifacts Get

get.description:
One artifact by ``produces:`` name; ``404`` when this node-step has none by that
name.

post.description: Record a worker's ask against its lease, minting the question id.
Record a worker's explicit artifact for ``request_body.name`` against its lease.
Record a lease's session-end, stamped with the injected clock.

get.description: The lease's parsed transcript — 404 iff no lease with this id ever existed.

The runner's machine-local summary: identity, pause states, capacities, hub
connectivity, last tick (issue #51).

Read-only over its wiring (``bzh:controller-read-only``): the edge holds only the
composition-root-wired :class:`RunnerStatusService`, derived entirely from local
store facts plus the injected clock — no hub call, so it is truthful with the hub
unreachable. On the store-free app (OpenAPI export / unit tests) the service is
unwired and the probe answers 503 rather than pretending.

patch.description:
Set this runner's own pause brake — it starts no new workers (issue #45).

Local to this machine and independent of the hub's brake: it works with the hub
unreachable, and it neither reads nor writes the hub's flag. Every spawn site honors
it — FILL, restart-resume, an answer-resume, ADVANCE's next-node, a requeue or
claim-adopt respawn, and the judgement resume that elicits a verdict from an exited
worker's session. REAP still reaps an orphan lease (nothing to kill, and its respawn
is itself suppressed), but does not kill a worker that is merely stalled — pause is
not a drain. Escalating a chunk to a human at an exhausted retry budget is deferred
too, wherever it would happen (REAP, a rejected flush at PULL): a paused runner does
not hand work off as unrecoverable while it waits. No retry is consumed at all: the
budget counts lease mints, and the one mint site sits below the gate.

A worker that *exits* while paused is **not** judged until the brake clears — judging
it resumes its session headlessly, which is itself a spawn the brake forbids. It waits
exactly like a suppressed respawn: the lease stays active, and ADVANCE retries the
judgement every tick until the brake clears. A live worker already running is left
alone throughout — this is not a drain, and it does not kill. Leases, routes, epochs,
environments, and retry budgets are otherwise unchanged.

post.description: Mint a selftest run against ``harness`` and begin it off the request thread.

get.description: Read back a selftest run's current state.
Every takeover still open — the recovery surface for a stranded one.
The effective spawn preamble prompt: the runtime override if set, else static config (issue #17).

put.description: Replace the runtime workspace-prompt override — effective on subsequent spawns (issue #17).
